concepts.lesson: The core message is that success often requires multiple attempts. Giving up after a single failure prevents growth and achievement.
concepts.real_example: A student failing a math test but studying harder to pass the next one instead of quitting.

concepts.concept_name: Courage in the Face of Failure
concepts.lesson: It takes courage to face the possibility of failing again. True bravery is appearing back at the task after a setback.
concepts.real_example: An athlete falling during a race but getting back up to finish the lap.

concepts.lesson: The poem teaches that there is no 'disgrace' in failing as long as one continues to strive. Shame only lies in stopping.
concepts.real_example: Losing a debate competition but realizing the experience made you a better speaker.
key_points: The difference between losing and quitting

concepts.lesson: If others can achieve a task through hard work, there is no reason why you cannot achieve it with the same patience.
concepts.real_example: Observing a classmate master a difficult craft and realizing you can too if you practice as much.
key_points: The universality of the 'Try Again' rule

concepts.lesson: Time is a critical factor in success. Rushing leads to frustration; waiting and working steadily ensures the 'reward' is reached.
concepts.real_example: Gardening requires waiting for seeds to sprout; you cannot force the plant to grow faster.

concepts.lesson: The process of trying again repeatedly builds a resilient character that can handle any future life challenges.
concepts.real_example: A person who has faced many rejections becomes a calm and effective problem-solver in adulthood.
key_points: Building confidence through survival of failure
